_meta.purpose: Fetch-verified sweep answering: does anything already score an agent on VERIFYING a spec claim against a black box via efficient probing? Grounds the Witness Suite build decision.
_meta.verdict: Provisionally underserved. Closest neighbors (PBT-Bench, TestExplora) have spec+black-box+bug-catching but score detection only — none score probe economy/minimality. Mainstream (SWE-bench family, METR suites, SWE-RL/RLVR) measure production of code, not audit of claims. PROVISIONAL: deeper looks at PBT-Bench/TestExplora follow-ups or ISSTA/FSE 2026 could close the gap.
_meta.rule: All findings fetched by the sweep agent. Credit the neighbors; do not overclaim novelty.

findings.title: SWE-bench / SWE-bench Verified
findings.says: Agent produces a patch for a GitHub issue; graded by pre-written hidden tests (FAIL->PASS). Agent never decides what to test or probes a black box.

findings.title: METR task suites (HCAST, RE-Bench, SWAA)
findings.says: ~250 tasks measuring autonomous task-completion time horizons; no spec-vs-implementation audit tasks.

findings.title: PBT-Bench
findings.says: Docs-as-spec + injected-bug library versions; agent writes Hypothesis property tests to catch bugs (100 problems, 365 bugs). Scores bug recall only — no probe-economy metric.

findings.title: TestExplora
findings.says: 2,389 tasks, 482 repos; docs as oracle; find latent bugs in black-box repos (best models ~16% F2P). Scored on discovery, not probe minimality.

findings.title: SWE-RL (representative of RLVR training trend)
findings.says: RL with deterministic rewards on PR history — verifiable means the REWARD is deterministic, not that the agent verifies third-party work.

findings.title: Anthropic: Demystifying evals for AI agents
findings.says: Evals OF agents (graders judge agent output) — not agents performing audits of another system.

findings.title: ORACLE benchmark
findings.says: Black-box hidden-function discovery via bounded exploration — function discovery, not spec-conformance; efficiency noted but not scored.

findings.title: AgentEval / workflow-graph boundary testing
findings.says: Auto-mines behavioral boundaries of conversational agents; scored on coverage/false alarms, not probe count, and no pre-written explicit spec.
